---
title: "Schedule"
author: "Davi Moreira"
editor: visual
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .overflow-table { font-size: 0.85rem; width: 100%; line-height: 1.2; }
  .overflow-table th, .overflow-table td {
    padding: 0.3rem; text-align: left; word-wrap: break-word; vertical-align: top;
  }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(1), .overflow-table td:nth-child(1) { width: 4%; }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(2), .overflow-table td:nth-child(2) { width: 9%; }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(5), .overflow-table td:nth-child(5) { width: 12%; }
  .below-table { font-size: 0.85rem; line-height: 1.2; margin-top: 1rem; }
</style>
```

# Course Schedule

Forty-four Monday/Wednesday/Friday meetings (42 in person, 2 asynchronous
online). The weekly rhythm: **Monday and Wednesday are lectures** (new content,
one notebook per topic), and **every Friday is a studio** — a quick recap of
the week, the next project milestone presented, and the rest of the class spent
working on your milestone and research project. Open each notebook in Colab
from its badge; milestone instructions and rubrics are on Brightspace. Topic
resources are also cataloged on the [Material](material.qmd) page, and every
dataset the course uses is in the
[course datasets (.zip)](notebooks/data/honr46400_datasets.zip) bundle.


::: overflow-table

| # | Date | Topic | Notebook | Milestone | Materials |
|---|------|-------|----------|-----------|-----------|
| | | **Phase 1 — Curiosity, gaps, answerable questions** | | | |
| 1 | Mon Aug 24 | Launchpad: How do we know? Course map, Colab, and the Ask → Verify → Document policy *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb00_launchpad_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M00 | RDSS ch. 1 (Preface) + ch. 2 'What is a research design?' — skim before Wednesday |
| 2 | Wed Aug 26 | From curiosity to a topic, and the gap worth filling *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb01_curiosity_to_question_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M00 | RDSS ch. 2 'What is a research design?' + ch. 3 'Research design principles' |
| 3 | Fri Aug 28 | Studio Friday — Curiosity pitches: M00 due, M01 kickoff | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb01_curiosity_to_question_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M00 | — |
| 4 | Mon Aug 31 | From problem to an answerable question *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb01_curiosity_to_question_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M01 | RDSS ch. 4 'Getting started' |
| | | **Phase 2 — Compass, sources, model & measurement** | | | |
| 5 | Wed Sep 2 | The inquiry compass: kind (descriptive vs causal) × reach (data, population, unseen) *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb02_four_approaches_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M01 | RDSS ch. 7 'Defining the inquiry' (descriptive vs causal inquiries) + Part III opener<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 6 | Fri Sep 4 | Studio Friday — Question pitches: M01 due, M02 kickoff | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb02_four_approaches_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M01 | — |
| 7 | Wed Sep 9 | Sources with integrity: search, verify, and map what a field actually claims *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb03_sources_claims_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M02 | RDSS ch. 4 'Getting started' |
| 8 | Fri Sep 11 | Studio Friday — Claim-map gallery: M02 due, M03 kickoff | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb03_sources_claims_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M02 | — |
| 9 | Mon Sep 14 | Model & Inquiry (MIDA I): the world your question lives in, and the exact quantity you seek *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb04_model_inquiry_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M03 | RDSS ch. 6 'Specifying the model' + ch. 7 'Defining the inquiry'; declaration_7.1 |
| 10 | Wed Sep 16 | Measurement & operationalization: from concept to indicator, and what error does to answers *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb05_measurement_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M03 | RDSS ch. 8 'Crafting a data strategy' — measurement sections |
| 11 | Fri Sep 18 | Studio Friday — Model & Inquiry declarations: M03 due, M04 kickoff | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb04_model_inquiry_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M03 | — |
| | | **Phase 3 — Data & answer strategies** | | | |
| 12 | Mon Sep 21 | Description deep dive: honest summaries and truthful charts (EDA anchor) *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb06_description_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M04 | RDSS ch. 15 'Observational: descriptive'<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 13 | Wed Sep 23 | Data strategy I (MIDA II): sampling — who gets into your data? *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb07_data_strategy_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M04 | RDSS ch. 8 'Crafting a data strategy' §8.1 sampling; declaration_2.1 |
| 14 | Fri Sep 25 | Studio Friday — measurement walkthroughs: M04 presents, M05 kicks off | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb07_data_strategy_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M04 | — |
| 15 | Mon Sep 28 | Data strategy II: assignment, feasibility, and the ethics checkpoint *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb07_data_strategy_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M05 | RDSS ch. 8 §assignment sections |
| 16 | Wed Sep 30 | Answer strategy I (MIDA III): estimators — how data become an answer *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb09_answer_strategy_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M05 | RDSS ch. 9 'Choosing an answer strategy'; declaration_9.1 |
| 17 | Fri Oct 2 | **ASYNC** — Diagnose a misleading claim, then your own data strategy (M05 submits) | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb08_async_claim_diagnosis_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M05 | RDSS ch. 9 regression/estimator sections<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 18 | Mon Oct 5 | Answer strategy II: regression as an estimator (not an oracle) *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb09_answer_strategy_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M06 | RDSS ch. 9 regression/estimator sections |
| 19 | Wed Oct 7 | Generalization deep dive I: uncertainty, and how far the answer reaches *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb10_inference_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M07 | RDSS ch. 9–10 uncertainty sections; ch. 15 on observational reach |
| 20 | Fri Oct 9 | Studio Friday — the URC abstract gate: workshop, M06 clinic, declaration assembly begins | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb10_inference_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M07 | — |
| 21 | Wed Oct 14 | Generalization deep dive II: power, and the association-is-not-causation boundary *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb10_inference_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M06 | RDSS ch. 10 'Diagnosing designs' §power; ch. 15–16 boundary sections; problem set 1<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 22 | Fri Oct 16 | Studio Friday — full design declaration due: assembly consults, M08 kicks off | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb10_inference_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M07 | — |
| | | **Phase 4 — Pilot, diagnosis, redesign** | | | |
| 23 | Mon Oct 19 | The diagnosis engine I: declare your design in code and interrogate it *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb11_declare_diagnose_redesign_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M08 | RDSS ch. 10 'Diagnosing designs'; declaration_10.1; ch. 13 'Designing in code' |
| 24 | Wed Oct 21 | The diagnosis engine II: redesign — fix it before you run it *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb11_declare_diagnose_redesign_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M08 | RDSS ch. 11 'Redesigning'; declaration_11.1 |
| 25 | Fri Oct 23 | Studio Friday — redesign pitches: M08 due, the pilot phase begins | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb11_declare_diagnose_redesign_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M08 | — |
| 26 | Mon Oct 26 | Prediction deep dive: target, baseline, split, metric — and the leaks that fake success *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb12_prediction_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M09 | RDSS Part III stops before prediction (ch. 15–18) — nb12 authors the missing library entry 'Observational: predictive' in the book's own declare-diagnose-redesign format; ch. 9 §9.1.3 (the answer strategy is the whole procedure) + ch. 10 diagnosand logic carry over<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 27 | Wed Oct 28 | Causal reasoning deep dive: counterfactuals, randomization, and what earns the word 'because' *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb13_causal_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M09 | RDSS ch. 18 'Experimental: causal' + ch. 16 'Observational: causal' DiD/RDD/IV intuition sections; declaration_18.1 |
| 28 | Fri Oct 30 | Studio Friday — pilot walkthroughs: M09 due, poster sprint previewed | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb13_causal_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M09 | — |
| | | **Phase 5 — Poster construction & submission** | | | |
| 29 | Mon Nov 2 | Poster I: storyboard — one claim, one hierarchy of evidence *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb14_poster_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M10 | — |
| 30 | Wed Nov 4 | Poster II: draft + red-team — attack your own poster before strangers do *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb14_poster_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M11 | — |
| 31 | Fri Nov 6 | Studio Friday — final poster production and SUBMISSION: the deadline is today | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb14_poster_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M12 | — |
| | | **Phase 6 — Conference preparation** | | | |
| 32 | Mon Nov 9 | Delivery I: the 1-minute and 3-minute pitch architectures *(Lecture 1/3)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb15_communicating_evidence_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M13 | — |
| 33 | Wed Nov 11 | Delivery II: uncertainty and limitations, spoken like a scientist *(Lecture 2/3)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb15_communicating_evidence_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M13 | — |
| 34 | Fri Nov 13 | Studio Friday — the hot seat: your question bank under live fire | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb15_communicating_evidence_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M14 | — |
| 35 | Mon Nov 16 | Dress rehearsal: the full URC simulation — readiness audited *(Lecture 3/3)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb15_communicating_evidence_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M15 | — |
| — | Tue Nov 17 | **🎓 Purdue Fall Undergraduate Research Expo — REQUIRED poster presentation (M16)** | | M16 | URC Expo |
| | | **Phase 7 — Reflection, feedback, redesign** | | | |
| 36 | Wed Nov 18 | Conference debrief: what the audience taught you, and what you would redesign *(Lecture 1/1)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb16_debrief_redesign_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M17 | RDSS ch. 11 'Redesigning' |
| 37 | Fri Nov 20 | Studio Friday — redesign declared: feedback becomes the dossier work plan | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb16_debrief_redesign_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M18 | — |
| 38 | Mon Nov 23 | **ASYNC** — Poster-to-Dossier module: robustness, sensitivity, and the claim ledger | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb17_async_poster_to_dossier_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M19 | RDSS ch. 22 'Realization' — reporting what actually happened |
| | | **Phase 8 — Reproducibility, brief, Evidence Defense** | | | |
| 39 | Mon Nov 30 | Reproducibility I: could a stranger rebuild your findings from what you hand them? *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb18_reproducibility_brief_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M20 | RDSS ch. 21 'Planning' + ch. 22 'Realization' |
| 40 | Wed Dec 2 | Reproducibility II: sign-off, and the one-page research brief *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb18_reproducibility_brief_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M20 | RDSS ch. 22 (continued) + ch. 23 'Integration' opening |
| 41 | Fri Dec 4 | Studio Friday — the table read: every brief read by every margin, the defense staged | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb19_evidence_defense_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M21 | — |
| 42 | Mon Dec 7 | Evidence Defense I: defend your claims, your choices, and your verification *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb19_evidence_defense_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M22 | — |
| 43 | Wed Dec 9 | Evidence Defense II: the remaining defenses — and the dossier's final checklist *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb19_evidence_defense_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M22 | — |
| 44 | Fri Dec 11 | Studio Friday — the ceremony: one semester on the compass, dossiers in | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb19_evidence_defense_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M23 | — |

:::

::: below-table

**Key dates:** URC abstract internal gate **Fri Oct 9** · Final poster
**Fri Nov 6** · **Purdue Fall Undergraduate Research Expo: Tue Nov 17**
(required poster presentation) · Evidence Defenses **Dec 7 & 9** · Final
dossier **Fri Dec 11**. No class: Sep 7 (Labor Day), Oct 12 (October Break),
Nov 25/27 (Thanksgiving). Async-online meetings: Oct 2 and Nov 23.

## Core Course References

- **Blair, G., Coppock, A., & Humphreys, M.** (2023). *Research Design in the
  Social Sciences: Declaration, Diagnosis, and Redesign*. Princeton University
  Press. Read free online: [book.declaredesign.org](https://book.declaredesign.org/){target="_blank"}.
- **Bergstrom, C. T., & West, J. D.** (2020). *Calling Bullshit: The Art of
  Skepticism in a Data-Driven World* — optional companion; public case studies
  at [callingbullshit.org](https://callingbullshit.org/){target="_blank"}.
- Course datasets ship from the MIT-licensed `rdss` package (see
  [`notebooks/data/`](https://github.com/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/tree/main/notebooks/data){target="_blank"}).

:::
